parsed.summary: This commit bundles an npm scope rename (breaking change) with a setup-menu fix under a single 'fix' commit targeting v0.4.7. The scope rename is a consumer-breaking change that should not ship as a minor/patch version without migration tooling. Additionally, the new interactive menu in setup.md has no visible guard for non-interactive/CI environments, risking silent hangs or failures in scripted installations.

findings.title: npm scope rename is a breaking change shipped as a patch-level fix
findings.body: The commit message explicitly states 'rename npm scope,' and package.json shows 4 lines changed, consistent with updating the package name field and related references. .claude-plugin/marketplace.json (4 lines) and plugin.json (2 lines) also changed, likely updating scope-dependent identifiers. Renaming an npm scope breaks every existing import, install command, and dependency reference for current consumers. The CHANGELOG headers reference v0.4.7, a minor/patch bump, which semver would not cover a scope rename. There is no visible migration guide, codemod, or backward-compat shim in the diff. Consumers who pin or reference the old scope will get 404s from the registry after publication.
findings.recommendation: Separate the scope rename into its own commit at a minimum, and ideally a major version bump. Provide a migration guide and deprecation path (e.g., publish a stub package under the old scope that redirects or warns). Do not bundle with the setup-menu fix.

findings.title: Interactive setup menu likely lacks non-TTY / CI guard
findings.body: The commands/setup.md diff (19 lines changed) adds an interactive menu shown when the system detects an already-configured state. If this menu uses stdin prompts without checking for TTY availability or a --non-interactive / --yes flag, automated setups (CI pipelines, Docker builds, scripted onboarding) will hang indefinitely waiting for input that never arrives. The diff stat shows no companion test file or guard logic, and markdown-based command definitions in this framework typically execute as-is. This is a classic silent-failure scenario in headless environments.
findings.recommendation: Inspect the rendered menu logic and ensure it either: (a) detects non-TTY and falls back to a safe default (e.g., reconfigure = no), or (b) accepts a flag/skip mechanism. Add an automated test or CI step that invokes the setup command in a non-interactive shell to confirm it does not hang.

findings.title: Large release_card.md rewrite (273 additions, 103 deletions) ships without validation
findings.body: release_card.md accounts for the bulk of the diff (326 lines changed). This file likely serves as the user-facing release description or marketplace listing. With a rewrite this large, any incorrect instructions, broken markdown links, stale version references, or copy-paste errors will be directly user-visible at release time. There is no indication of validation (link checking, spell-check, preview step) in the diff or commit metadata.
findings.recommendation: Before merge, render the release_card.md content and verify all links, code snippets, version numbers, and scope references are correct and consistent with the new npm scope. Consider adding a CI lint/render step for this file.

next_steps: Verify the npm scope rename is intentional for this release and confirm the new scope is already registered and published on the npm registry.
Test the setup command in a non-interactive environment (pipe yes/no or run headless) to confirm it does not hang on the new interactive menu.
Split this commit: ship the setup-menu fix independently, then handle the scope rename with proper semver and migration documentation.
Render and proofread the full release_card.md before tagging v0.4.7.
